stories.title: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again
stories.simple_summary: Rich tech company leaders are jumping into AI projects again because they're afraid of missing out on the chance to make even more money from AI's growth.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech industry watchers — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Uber’s product chief on hotels, robotaxis, and why the company doesn’t want to be “everything for everyone”
stories.simple_summary: Uber is expanding beyond rides into hotels and self-driving cars, and is adding AI features that riders and drivers will start to notice soon.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Uber, you may see some new features and services appear over time, but nothing major is changing right now.

stories.title: Video-generation startup PixVerse raises $439M, valuation soars past $2B
stories.simple_summary: A company called PixVerse that makes AI videos just raised a huge amount of money and is now worth over 2 billion dollars.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — it shows AI video tools are getting a lot of funding, but doesn't directly affect your daily life yet.

stories.title: Hermes agent maker Nous Research in talks for new funding at $1.5B valuation
stories.simple_summary: A company called Nous Research that makes AI helpers is trying to raise at least $75 million from investors, making the company worth $1.5 billion.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly important to investors tracking AI companies — it doesn't affect you personally right now.

stories.title: Nobel economists, tech leaders warn how AI could threaten jobs
stories.simple_summary: Famous economists and tech leaders are warning that AI could cost people their jobs.
stories.why_it_matters: This is an important concern — if you own a small business, it's worth thinking about how AI might change your industry over the next few years.

stories.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
stories.simple_summary: Microsoft's leader Satya Nadella is warning companies about a potential risk with using AI from big tech companies — he thinks there may be security or control problems.
stories.why_it_matters: If your business uses AI tools, it's smart to ask your tech provider what safeguards they have in place, just like you'd check a contractor's insurance.

stories.title: Siri AI is already changing how I use my iPhone
stories.simple_summary: Apple's new iPhone software (iOS 27) includes AI improvements to Siri, the voice assistant, that are starting to be tested by regular users.
stories.why_it_matters: If you have an iPhone, you'll eventually get these updates and your Siri will work better — this is a practical improvement coming to your phone.

stories.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, claiming the company stole Apple's secret information and hired Apple employees to help them do it.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a legal battle between two big companies — mostly interesting to watch, but it doesn't directly affect you unless you work in tech.

stories.title: Anthropic’s Claude Science app is coming for Kendall Square
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic, the company that makes Claude (an AI assistant), is launching a science-focused app aimed at researchers and scientists.
stories.why_it_matters: Unless you work in science or research, this probably won't affect you — it's a specialized tool for professionals.

stories.title: Top House progressive warns Democrats to get serious on AI messaging
stories.simple_summary: A top Democratic politician is telling other Democrats they need better ways to talk about AI to the public and voters.
stories.why_it_matters: This is about political communication — it shows politicians are paying more attention to AI as an election issue.

stories.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
stories.simple_summary: Sam Altman from OpenAI responded to Elon Musk's criticism by pointing out that Musk is also making wild promises about future technology that aren't proven yet.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly famous tech people arguing with each other — nothing you need to worry about.

stories.title: Trump Administration Is Snapping Up Stakes in Private Companies. Could A.I. Be Next?
stories.simple_summary: The Trump administration is buying stakes in private companies and the question is whether they might do the same with AI companies.
stories.why_it_matters: This is about government investment policy — it's too early to know what this means, but it shows government is watching AI companies closely.
